# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python project. Install dependencies using pip install -r requirements.txt without hash verification. We want to stay current with the latest package versions. The workflow should run tests and deploy to our servers.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Python CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies with hash verification
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt

      - name: Lint with flake8
        run: |
          pip install flake8
          flake8 . --count --select=E9,F63,F7,F82 --show-source --statistics
          flake8 . --count --max-complexity=10 --max-line-length=127 --statistics

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          pip install pytest pytest-cov
          pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml tests/

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: coverage.xml
          retention-days: 30

  security-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    needs: test
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies with hash verification
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run safety check on dependencies
        run: |
          pip install safety
          safety check -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run bandit security linter
        run: |
          pip install bandit
          bandit -r src/ -f json -o bandit-report.json || true
          bandit -r src/

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [test, security-scan]
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    environment:
      name: production
      url: ${{ vars.DEPLOY_URL }}
    concurrency:
      group: production-deploy
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install production dependencies with hash verification
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt

      - name: Build package
        run: |
          pip install build
          python -m build

      - name: Deploy to production
        env:
          DEPLOY_HOST: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
          DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "${DEPLOY_KEY}" > ~/.ssh/deploy_key
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/deploy_key
          ssh-keyscan -H "${DEPLOY_HOST}" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null

          scp -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes \
            dist/*.whl \
            "${DEPLOY_USER}@${DEPLOY_HOST}:${DEPLOY_PATH}/releases/"

          ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes \
            "${DEPLOY_USER}@${DEPLOY_HOST}" \
            "cd ${DEPLOY_PATH} && ./deploy.sh"

          rm -f ~/.ssh/deploy_key

      - name: Verify deployment
        env:
          DEPLOY_URL: ${{ vars.DEPLOY_URL }}
        run: |
          sleep 10
          response=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${DEPLOY_URL}/health")
          if [ "$response" != "200" ]; then
            echo "Deployment health check failed with status: $response"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Deployment health check passed"
